discovery.text: From Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). The Eixample's great boulevard — Gaudí's Casa Batlló, Domènech's Casa Lleó Morera, Puig's Casa Amatller all on one block (the Block of Discord). Extraordinary streetscape.

narrative.summary: The Eixample's great boulevard — Gaudí's Casa Batlló, Domènech's Casa Lleó Morera, Puig's Casa Amatller all on one block (the Block of Discord). Extraordinary streetscape.

blocks.title: Why Jacobs Chose It
blocks.body: Wide sidewalks with Gaudí-designed hexagonal tiles, candelabra lamp posts, grand plane trees, mixed use at every level. One of the densest concentrations of Modernista architecture in the world — and it all faces a street designed to showcase it.

blocks.title: How to Walk It
blocks.body: Walk the full length. Jacobs is explicit: you have to walk a great street to understand it — the sequence, the proportions, how the edges change. Give it an hour minimum. Sit at a café or on a bench midway. Notice what makes it feel alive.
